synthesis.default_mechanism_summary: Defaults on the E2E path are chosen by at least eight uncoordinated mechanisms across nine files, with no single registry mapping stage -> chosen tech/weights/policy. (1) Hardcoded module-level constants in process_video.py: DEFAULT_WASB_CHECKPOINT:176, DEFAULT_WASB_REPO:177, DEFAULT_CONFIDENCE_CALIBRATION_CURVES:178 (a stale dated run dir), DEFAULT_MESH_COVERAGE_MODE:179='ball_aware', DEFAULT_TARGET_MESH_FRAME_BUDGET:180=100, DEFAULT_REID_MODEL:175, DEFAULT_GLOBAL_ASSOCIATION_PROFILE:252 (an internal-val-tuned profile). (2) Dataclass field defaults (PipelineOptions / RemoteConfig / PlacementConfig / BodyPostChainConfig / CameraMotionOptions) that usually re-state the constant but sometimes DIVERGE — the BODY case ships two contradictory defaults for one stage (orchestrator.py:657-658 yolo/moge2 vs remote_body_dispatch.py:236-244 ''/''). (3) argparse CLI defaults (mostly = the constant). (4) module-level constants in other modules consumed as defaults: worldhmr.py:48 (grounding anchor) with a duplicate at orchestrator.py:94, camera_motion.py:17 (auto-threshold 2.5), frame_rating.py:53-54 (proximity/swing floors), ball_arc_solver.py:148 (fit_spin_scalar=False, kill-pinned). (5) models/MANIFEST.json id+checksum lookup — but this is PROVENANCE ONLY, used to verify the yolo26m person detector and fast_sam assets and to record the WASB hash (TECH_BLUEPRINTS:869 'the ONLY place this hash is recorded'); it does NOT select the ball or court checkpoint, and the three trained candidates (stage1_official, seed_official, court_unet_v2) are absent from it entirely, so there is no surface on which a promotion could even be recorded as a default flip. (6) A single-file yaml pin for the tracker (orchestrator.py:92 botsort_reid.yaml; 5 of 6 presets dead). (7) Passive file-presence gates that consume-if-present but never produce by default (court_detector_v2_proposals.json; racket_pose_estimate.json). (8) SERVER-LAYER defaults that OVERRIDE the CLI defaults — server/gpu_runner.py GpuRunRequest and server/worker/daemon.py hardcode allow_auto_court_corners_preview=True, so the real product upload path resolves DIFFERENT defaults than a bare CLI run (and silently runs the inferior classic-CV court path). Plus fleet-ops prose conventions (gpu_fleet.md H100 SKU) that are not code-enforced. Net fragmentation: the same default is defined in 2-3 places and can drift; four model choices use four different selection idioms; a genuinely flagless run cannot even complete (court calibration hard-fails without a source; BODY remote hard-degrades without --remote-host), so 'the default E2E run' already means a minimal flag set plus whatever the server layer injects on top.
synthesis.manifest_design_inputs: A best_stack manifest must become the SINGLE selection surface every stage reads for 'which tech/weights/policy is default,' replacing the scattered constants and dataclass defaults, and it must cover every selection point the audit found: (1) Ball 2D detector checkpoint (today process_video.py:176 hardcoded) with a promotion field keyed to a held-out ledger row, so seed_official can be flipped in by editing one manifest entry rather than source. (2) Court calibration method + neural court checkpoint (court_unet_v2, currently unregistered — must be ADDED to the registry) + court_detector_v2 fusion enable, each carrying its named gate (PCK@5px 0.95, calv1_fusedefault). (3) Person detector (yolo26m — already MANIFEST-driven, keep) + tracker yaml (botsort_reid.yaml) + global-association profile, distinguishing the intended PRODUCTION default from the eval-only per-clip overrides. (4) ReID checkpoint. (5) BODY detector + FOV model — MUST reconcile the orchestrator/RemoteConfig divergence and pin a FOV path that exists on the live VM (the moge_2 stale /workspace path is the active bug). (6) Mesh scheduling: coverage mode (ball_aware) + frame-budget-vs-byte-budget (owner-ruled byte-budget PRIMARY, still opt-in) + a tier-eligibility knob (byte-budget alone does not densify wolverine-class clips). (7) BODY post-chain preset (default vs raw) + the six per-stage toggles. (8) Confidence-gate calibration-curves pointer — currently a stale dated dir; the manifest must own a refreshable pointer with a re-verify cadence (Part IV rule 3). (9) Camera-motion policy (auto-threshold vs opt-in — resolve the cross-audit contradiction and pin one). (10) Ball-arc solver knobs: fit_spin_scalar=False (kill-pinned) and the protected-span baseline (eval-clip-scoped only — mark it so it is never mistaken for a general default). (11) Paddle fused estimator enable (P3-1, fail-closed/ESTIMATED band). (12) Remote host / transport (tar_batch) / SKU. (13) The SERVER-LAYER defaults (gpu_runner GpuRunRequest, worker daemon) MUST be brought under the same manifest so a bare-CLI run and a product-server run resolve identical defaults — today they diverge on auto-court-corners. Each entry needs: current value + file:line it lives at today, the chosen tech, a trust band, a promotion-gate reference (ledger row / named gate), and a wired? boolean. Two hard invariants: (a) a manifest entry is a DEFAULT selection, never a VERIFIED claim (VERIFIED=0 stays 0 until a product gate passes on real labels); (b) killed/dormant items (Magnus S, physics-gated teacher, Fast-body, body_array_native, binary-chunk transport, TrackNetV4/V5, warm-worker daemon, the dead tracker presets) must appear as explicitly-dormant entries citing their kill/ruling, NOT as gaps to fill.

Concurrent-session caution honored: court_detector_v2*.py and court_model_infer.py have uncommitted working-tree edits (per git status) but were not needed for this BALL audit and were not read from the dirty tree. Single most important finding for the owner directive ("every gain must be wired into the default E2E run, nothing orphaned"): the raw, non-fine-tuned WASB-tennis zero-shot checkpoint remains the sole default ball detector in a plain process_video.py invocation. Every trained candidate from waves 3-6 (P1-1 warm-start, stage1_official, seed_official) exists only as banked lane artifacts under runs/lanes/w5_ballretrain_20260707/ and runs/lanes/w6_labelingest_20260708/gpu_rescore/ with zero code path back to the E2E default (no CLI flag even exposes them — a caller would have to hand-edit --wasb-checkpoint to point at a lane directory). This is by design, not oversight: NORTH_STAR_ROADMAP.md and BUILD_CHECKLIST.md repeatedly assert 'VERIFIED=0 unchanged' and gate promotion on (a) beating the 0.7248 held-out anchor on a PRE-REGISTERED strict held-out row in runs/manager/heldout_eval_ledger.md, and (b) an explicit owner go, neither of which has happened. The wave-6 close-errand re-score (freshest evidence, same day as this audit) is a striking reversal not yet reflected in any BUILD_CHECKLIST bullet: on the new 20-clip owner-label LoSO scoring, seed_official (LoSO-mean F1@20 0.5584) beats both official_tennis_control (0.3033, the E2E default's own architecture/base) and stage1_official (0.2666, wave-5's previously-declared winner) by a wide margin — computed directly from runs/lanes/w6_labelingest_20260708/gpu_rescore/loso/loso_report.json's candidates.*.loso_mean_metrics.label_f1_at_20px. This result exists only as a raw JSON artifact; the loso_report.json itself is stamped ball_verified:false and heldout_comparison_count:0, and no BUILD_CHECKLIST bullet after the dispatch (line 767) and adjudication (line 769) entries rules on it as of HEAD — it is banked, unruled, unpromoted evidence, exactly the "orphaned gain" pattern the owner directive is watching for, though the orphaning here is process-compliant (correctly withheld pending the pre-registered held-out gate), not a bug.

BVP span v2 and rally gating are the two BALL-chain gains from this window that ARE fully wired into the default E2E path with no flag required. Magnus spin and physics/2D-gated teacher selection are both deliberately dormant/killed by measured negative results, correctly reflected in code (fit_spin_scalar=False constant; no consensus-fusion/gated-teacher code path exists in the default chain).

The WASB ball-tracking checkpoint used in a plain no-flag run is hard-coded to DEFAULT_WASB_CHECKPOINT (process_video.py:176), which resolves to models/checkpoints/wasb/wasb_tennis_best.pth.tar — verified on disk to be a genuine standalone file (not a symlink), matching the pretrained-tennis entry in models/MANIFEST.json (id: wasb_tennis_bmvc2023, fine_tuned_on_pickleball: false). The prior auditor's WIRED_DEFAULT verdict is CONFIRMED, but the cited mechanism is imprecise: orchestrator.py:86 is merely the import line for build_body_artifacts_from_fast_sam/compute_body_skeleton_and_metrics, not a call site, and worldhmr.py:48's R3_GROUNDING_ANCHOR_SOURCE constant is not actually what drives the default — it's a fallback used only inside worldhmr.compute_body_skeleton_and_metrics's own `grounding_anchor_source or (...)` expression, which is short-circuited because orchestrator.py always passes an explicit, non-None grounding_anchor_source. The real gating logic lives in orchestrator.py:826-833: it independently computes grounding_anchor_source = R3_GROUNDING_ANCHOR_SOURCE (a duplicate constant defined at orchestrator.py:94) when stance_index is non-empty AND tracks.placement_provenance is set, else falls back to DEFAULT_GROUNDING_ANCHOR_SOURCE ("track_world_xy"). Both preconditions hold in a plain no-flag E2E run: (1) the "placement" stage is unconditionally in process_video.py's prefix stage list (process_video.py:494-498) and always rewrites tracks.json in place with a populated placement_provenance block (placement.py:693, schema field at schemas/__init__.py:461); (2) stance detection (placement.py:2452 _detect_stance_frames) runs internally from placement-track speed with no external stance-phases file required, so stance_index is non-empty whenever a player has >=2 consecutive low-speed frames (near-universal in real rally footage — serves, waits, etc.); (3) the "body" stage is unconditionally in the stage list (process_video.py:545) and BodyStageRunner.run() re-reads the just-rewritten tracks.json (orchestrator.py:1166) before computing stance_index/grounding_anchor_source and passing them explicitly into whichever of the three BODY joint-builder branches fires (array-native, legacy default, or shared-compute) — all three pass stance_index and grounding_anchor_source explicitly (orchestrator.py:1169,1200,1212), and the default --body-postchain=default selects the branch that reaches worldhmr.compute stance-aware gate. Downstream, worldhmr.py:349-351 computes stance_aware_grounding = (anchor_source == R3_GROUNDING_ANCHOR_SOURCE and stance_index is not None), true under these conditions, and dispatches to _smooth_grounded_frames_stance_aware (worldhmr.py:366, function body at :2653), a real non-stub implementation reached from no other call site. No CLI flag, env var, or config knob is required to activate this in a standard process_video.py invocation.

verdicts.explanation: Verified by tracing the real invocation path at HEAD (camera_motion.py and process_video.py both have zero uncommitted changes per `git status --short`, so HEAD == working tree for these files). The "camera_motion" stage is unconditionally wired into every pipeline run via _build_prefix_stage_fns (process_video.py:493-498) -- not behind any flag gating stage inclusion. Inside _stage_camera_motion, a plain no-flag invocation has camera_motion_path=None, skip_camera_motion=False, and enable_camera_motion=False, which routes execution to the `else` branch (line 1256): it runs estimate_camera_motion_probe with threshold=CAMERA_MOTION_AUTO_THRESHOLD (2.5, imported from camera_motion.py:17), and if probe.enabled is True (motion_score > 2.5) it proceeds to the full estimate_camera_motion() call and writes camera_motion.json (line 1344-1345) with zero required CLI flags. No CLI flag exists to override the 2.5 threshold, so the code-level default from camera_motion.py:17 is what actually governs production runs. This is a genuine auto-on-by-motion-score design (img1605-style clips with real camera pan/shake exceed 2.5 and get compensation automatically; static clips don't) -- exactly matching the claim. verdicts.explanation: Traced the full invocation chain at HEAD (no uncommitted changes on these three files — `git status --short` was empty for all of them, so HEAD == working tree here). In process_video.py, `--body-postchain` argparse default is "default" (line 5101-5104: choices=("default","raw"), default="default"); `body_postchain_raw = args.body_postchain == "raw"` (4763) is therefore False on a plain run, and every postchain stage flag (`body_temporal_smoothing`, `body_foot_lock`, `body_foot_pin`, `body_contact_splice`, `body_world_joint_visual_smoothing`, `sam3d_wrist_bone_lock`) is computed as `not (no_body_X_flag or body_postchain_raw)` (4792-4799, 4786-4790), i.e. True by default since all `--no-body-*` store_true flags also default False. The identical pattern is mirrored verbatim in remote_body_dispatch.py's own CLI parser (2464-2469 default="default", 2470-2475 no_body_* default False) and RemoteConfig construction (2531, 2569-2577), and the RemoteConfig dataclass field itself defaults to `body_postchain_mode: str = "default"` (line 261) — no divergent default anywhere in the chain. Checked every construction site of `BodyPostChainConfig` repo-wide (orchestrator.py:713, worldhmr.py:322): both build it strictly from the passed-through CLI-derived booleans; `BodyPostChainConfig`'s own dataclass defaults are all True (body_postchain.py:22-27), and `.raw()` (29-38) is an explicit alternate constructor that is never called implicitly by any code path — only reachable by an explicit `--body-postchain raw` or an explicit `--no-body-*` flag. Grepped repo-wide for any config file, env var (BODY_POSTCHAIN), or MANIFEST.json reference that could flip the default silently — none exists. This fully confirms the prior auditor's claim: the knob is instrument-only, opt-in, and does not alter the default E2E pipeline behavior.

Repo-wide grep for `build_paddle_pose_fused` (both `_from_file` and `_from_skeleton`) shows callers only in tests/racketsport/test_paddle_pose_fused.py and the manual CLI scripts/racketsport/build_paddle_pose_fused.py:116. process_video.py's four cited lines (2982, 2996, 3002, 3053, 3078) all only construct a path to clip_dir/"racket_pose_estimate.json" and pass it through `_read_optional_json`/`.is_file()` checks into build_virtual_world_state and apply_confidence_gate_to_world — pure conditional reads, never a build call. orchestrator.py:2728 mirrors this with `_existing_file(context.run_dir / "racket_pose_estimate.json")`, also read-only. Checked all three refutation vectors the prompt asked about: (1) no config-file/env/MANIFEST.json trigger — grep of configs/racketsport/*.* and models/MANIFEST.json for paddle_pose_fused/racket_pose_estimate returns nothing; (2) no downstream-default or lane-runner subprocess call — grep for literal string "build_paddle_pose_fused.py" outside the CLI script itself and its test hits only documentation files (BUILD_CHECKLIST.md, NORTH_STAR_ROADMAP.md, TECH_STACK.md, TECH_BLUEPRINTS.md), never a caller; (3) no VM-side/remote-dispatch default — scripts/racketsport/remote_body_dispatch.py contains zero occurrences of "paddle_pose_fused" or "racket_pose_estimate" at all, so the GPU dispatch path doesn't build it either. Net effect confirmed: a plain no-flag E2E run of process_video.py will only pick up racket_pose_estimate.json if some other process (i.e., the manual CLI) already wrote it into the clip dir beforehand; nothing in the pipeline itself invokes the fused estimator. Prior auditor's claim stands unmodified.

areas.notes:
Scope correction up front: the task's stated entry points (threed/racketsport/orchestrator.py, threed/racketsport/remote_body_dispatch.py) mis-locate remote_body_dispatch.py — it actually lives at scripts/racketsport/remote_body_dispatch.py (2633 lines), not under threed/racketsport/. All citations above use the corrected path.

Concurrent-session caution followed: court_detector_v2.py, court_detector_v2_hypotheses.py, and court_model_infer.py showed as modified in the initial git status snapshot, but by the time I inspected them `git status --short` for those three paths was clean and `git log` showed they were landed by commits 4e1dc1350 and ca764a4d6 (the latter is HEAD) during this session — i.e. the concurrent court-calibration session's work merged to main while this audit was running. I verified via `git diff --stat` (empty) and `git log --oneline -3 -- threed/racketsport/court_model_infer.py` that HEAD already contains this code, so no working-tree-only content was treated as landed.

Biggest structural finding for the owner's "nothing orphaned" directive: the newly-trained court_unet_v2 neural checkpoint (todays's commit 7c412f7) has a clean inference adapter and geometric solver already committed at HEAD, but process_video.py's own --allow-auto-court-corners-preview path still calls the OLD classical white-line detector (threed/racketsport/court_line_keypoints.py), not court_model_infer.py — the new neural model is reachable only through the standalone scripts/racketsport/run_court_detector_v2.py CLI and is not registered in models/MANIFEST.json at all. process_video.py does have a *consumption*-side gate for a 'court_detector_v2_proposals.json' artifact (_detector_v2_correction_gate_before_tracking), but nothing on the default path produces that file.

Second structural finding: RemoteConfig's BODY detector/FOV defaults (body_detector_name='', body_fov_name='') deliberately diverge from orchestrator.BodyStageRunner's own constructor defaults (detector_name='yolo', fov_name='moge2') — documented in a code comment at remote_body_dispatch.py:225-244 as a forced workaround because the moge_2_vitl_normal FOV checkpoint's models/MANIFEST.json local_path is a stale H100 /workspace path that doesn't exist on the currently-reachable VM. This means the two 'defaults' shipped in this repo for the same stage actively disagree, and the in-process default is known-broken on the one host actually wired to receive the default remote dispatch.

Third: a genuinely flagless `process_video.py --video X` invocation cannot complete — the court-calibration stage raises _HardStageFailure unless at least one of --court-corners/--capture-sidecar/--court-calibration/--allow-auto-court-corners-preview/--court-proposals-preview is given, and even past that, BODY dispatch defaults to remote with RemoteConfig.host='' which raises via _require_remote_host unless --remote-host is supplied (or --no-gpu/--body-local chosen). So "the default E2E path" in practice always means some minimal flag set (at minimum a calibration source, and in practice --remote-host pointing at whatever VM is currently live per runs/manager/gpu_fleet.md) — worth the manager confirming what the actual "default no-flag" contract is meant to be before treating any of the above as a promotion blocker.

Fourth: 5 of 6 configs/racketsport/*.yaml tracker presets (all but botsort_reid.yaml) and configs/racketsport/ball_pretrain_roboflow_wasb.json are not read by any production code path — they are either historical ablation presets or training-time-only configs, not alternate default candidates reachable from process_video.py.

Did not audit: models/MANIFEST.json entries for stages I did not trace end-to-end (e.g. sam2_hiera_base_plus/grounding_dino racket-detection chain, mujoco_mjx physics) beyond confirming their manifest presence; a full racket/physics config-surface pass would need a separate dedicated read of threed/racketsport/racket_*.py and threed/racketsport/body_grounding_refine.py call sites. Also did not independently re-verify every sha256/local_path pin in tests/racketsport/test_model_manifest.py against the live models/MANIFEST.json byte-for-byte beyond the entries quoted above (fast_sam_3d_body_dinov3, tracknetv3, tracknetv3_inpaintnet, wasb_tennis_bmvc2023, grounding_dino_tiny_paddle_detector, rtmw-family retirement) — those matched on inspection.

verdicts.evidence_file_line: scripts/racketsport/process_video.py:1-4930 (no `from threed.racketsport.court_detector_v2 import` / no `court_model_infer` / no `court_unet` string anywhere in the file); scripts/racketsport/process_video.py:806-821+4157-4169 (the only other candidate route, `--court-proposals-preview` → `_court_proposals_preview_from_video` → `build_court_proposal_report`, is itself opt-in via CLI flag `court_proposals_preview: bool = False` at line 313 / `--court-proposals-preview` at line 4899, AND even when activated it only imports `threed.racketsport.court_line_bank.build_line_bank_from_image` + `threed.racketsport.court_regulation_proposals.propose_regulation_courts_from_line_bank` per scripts/racketsport/build_court_proposals.py:14-20 — classical geometry, never touching `court_detector_v2_hypotheses`/`court_model_infer`); threed/racketsport/court_proposals.py:640-643 (the actual neural bridge, `generate_homography_hypotheses`→`court_model_infer`, lives in court_proposals.py but is never imported by build_court_proposals.py or process_video.py); threed/racketsport/orchestrator.py and threed/racketsport/remote_body_dispatch.py (zero hits for court_detector_v2/court_model_infer/court_unet, ruling out a remote-dispatch or orchestrator-side default); models/MANIFEST.json (zero "court" matches, confirming no registry entry for the checkpoint)
verdicts.explanation: Traced the real invocation graph from the committed HEAD of scripts/racketsport/process_video.py (this file itself has no uncommitted changes, so it was read directly rather than via `git show HEAD:`). There is no import of the `court_detector_v2` module or `court_model_infer` anywhere in process_video.py, orchestrator.py, or remote_body_dispatch.py. The prior auditor's cited evidence holds: (1) `_auto_court_corners_preview_from_video` at line 4089 is gated behind `--allow-auto-court-corners-preview` and calls the classical `court_line_keypoints.detect_court_keypoints_from_image`, not the neural model. (2) I additionally traced the second, less obvious candidate route — `--court-proposals-preview` → `_court_proposals_preview_from_video` (line 4157) → `build_court_proposal_report` in scripts/racketsport/build_court_proposals.py — and confirmed it is ALSO opt-in (default False, requires the CLI flag) AND, even when opted in, only wires classical line-bank/regulation-court geometry (court_line_bank.py, court_regulation_proposals.py), never reaching court_detector_v2_hypotheses.generate_homography_hypotheses or court_model_infer, which is the only place in the codebase that actually loads the court_unet_v2 checkpoint by calling `make_court_model_infer_provider`. That neural bridge exists only in threed/racketsport/court_proposals.py:640-643, a module that is never imported by process_video.py or its build_court_proposals.py preview helper. (3) models/MANIFEST.json has no checkpoint entry for court_unet_v2/court_model_v2.pt, confirming no registry-driven default path either. No config file, env var, or VM/remote-dispatch default activates it — resolve_court_model_checkpoint_path() defaults to the real checkpoint path per tests/racketsport/test_court_fusion_default.py:50, but that function is only ever reached through detect_court_v2_from_frame(s)/court_detector_v2.py call sites, none of which are reachable from a plain no-flag process_video.py invocation. Verdict: CONFIRMED as BUILT_NOT_WIRED, no correction needed to the prior auditor's status.

verdicts.evidence_file_line: scripts/racketsport/process_video.py:186,946-948,1093-1119,4335-4337,4876-4878; scripts/racketsport/run_court_detector_v2.py:42
verdicts.explanation: The prior auditor's factual description of the mechanism is accurate, but "OPT_IN_FLAG" is the wrong enum. OPT_IN_FLAG implies there exists a CLI flag / env var / config knob that activates the gain on demand. There is none: process_video.py's argparse block (grepped in full; only --court-corners, --capture-sidecar, --court-keypoints relate to court calibration at all, lines 4876-4878) has zero mentions of detector_v2, and no flag causes process_video.py to invoke scripts/racketsport/run_court_detector_v2.py or to write court_detector_v2_proposals.json. The only producer of that file, confirmed by repo-wide git grep, is the standalone script scripts/racketsport/run_court_detector_v2.py:42 (plus test fixtures), which is never called from process_video.py, orchestrator.py, or remote_body_dispatch.py. I also checked models/MANIFEST.json and configs/racketsport/ for any detector_v2 default/knob — zero hits in both. So there is no route, flag-gated or otherwise, from a plain E2E process_video.py invocation to production of the proposal file that the gate needs to do anything. The gate itself (_detector_v2_correction_gate_before_tracking, lines 1093-1119) is a no-op in every default run: proposal_path.is_file() is False (line 1095) so it immediately returns None and tracking proceeds unaffected by this code at all. This matches BUILT_NOT_WIRED precisely: 'code exists (manual CLI / lane-local) but no path from the E2E entrypoint' — run_court_detector_v2.py is exactly that manual CLI, and process_video.py's consumption-side plumbing is unreachable dead weight absent someone manually running that separate script and dropping its output into the clip dir out-of-band. There is no flag a user could pass to process_video.py to turn this on; the only way to activate it is external orchestration outside the entrypoint, which is BUILT_NOT_WIRED's defining case, not OPT_IN_FLAG's.

verdicts.evidence_file_line: scripts/racketsport/remote_body_dispatch.py:226 (RemoteConfig.host: str = ""); scripts/racketsport/process_video.py:5058 (--remote-host default=RemoteConfig().host, i.e. ""); scripts/racketsport/process_video.py:4769-4770 (remote_config=RemoteConfig(host=args.remote_host,...)); scripts/racketsport/process_video.py:4854 (body_remote=not args.body_local -> True by default); scripts/racketsport/remote_body_dispatch.py:218-221 (_require_remote_host raises RemoteBodyDispatchError when host is blank); scripts/racketsport/remote_body_dispatch.py:1089-1090 (dispatch_body_stage calls _require_remote_host(config.host) before any SSH/reachability work); scripts/racketsport/process_video.py:2596-2597,2724-2758 (_dispatch_body_remote calls dispatch_body_stage inside a try/except RemoteBodyDispatchError that returns a StageOutcome(status="degraded") rather than crashing the whole process); scripts/fleet/refresh_remote_host.sh:5 ("Always pass the host explicitly to BODY dispatch") corroborates no default-host route exists anywhere.
verdicts.explanation: Traced the full path at HEAD and it matches the claimed evidence exactly. RemoteConfig.host defaults to "" (remote_body_dispatch.py:226), that default flows unchanged into process_video.py's --remote-host argparse default (line 5058) and into the RemoteConfig built at 4769-4770 from args.remote_host. body_remote defaults to True (line 4854: `body_remote=not args.body_local`, and args.body_local defaults False via store_true), so the BODY stage does take the remote dispatch branch (_dispatch_body_stage at 2596-2597) in a flagless run. dispatch_body_stage (remote_body_dispatch.py:1067) calls _require_remote_host(config.host) at line 1089-1090, before any SSH reachability check or real GPU work, so an empty host raises RemoteBodyDispatchError immediately -- no real GPU is ever reached without an explicit --remote-host. I searched for any alternate default-host route (env var, config file, MANIFEST, downstream/VM-side default) and found none; grep for REMOTE_HOST/env-var patterns turned up nothing, and the fleet operator scripts (scripts/fleet/refresh_remote_host.sh:5) explicitly document "Always pass the host explicitly to BODY dispatch," confirming there is no default-host path anywhere in the system. One correction to the prior auditor's phrasing (does not change the verdict): process_video.py does not "hard-fail" the whole invocation -- _dispatch_body_remote (process_video.py:2724-2758) catches RemoteBodyDispatchError and returns a StageOutcome(status="degraded") with notes explaining no fallback pose was generated, letting the rest of the pipeline continue rather than crashing outright. But the substantive claim -- that a flagless invocation never reaches a real GPU for BODY and requires an explicit --remote-host to activate -- is fully CONFIRMED, so OPT_IN_FLAG is the correct wiring status.

verdicts.gain_name: Untracked BoT-SORT/ByteTrack config variants (loose, no-reid, bytetrack)
verdicts.claimed_status: BUILT_NOT_WIRED — "only botsort_reid.yaml is ever referenced by production code ... the other 5 are not read by any non-test .py file in the repo ... dead/manual-only presets with no wiring or CLI flag surfacing them as alternatives"
verdicts.corrected_status: Mixed: botsort_reid_loose.yaml = OPT_IN_FLAG (functional via process_video.py --tracker-config); botsort_no_reid.yaml, botsort_no_reid_loose.yaml, bytetrack.yaml, bytetrack_loose.yaml = BUILT_NOT_WIRED through the E2E path (but for a different reason than claimed — they ARE read by non-test .py files and there IS a CLI flag, it's just that orchestrator's validator rejects them at runtime)
verdicts.evidence_file_line: scripts/racketsport/process_video.py:5139 (--tracker-config CLI flag, default=orchestrator.DEFAULT_BOTSORT_REID_CONFIG); threed/racketsport/orchestrator.py:513 (_verified_botsort_reid_config gate) and :2806-2818 (validator requires "tracker_type: botsort" + "with_reid: True"); threed/racketsport/mobile_person_yolo_replay.py:1237-1246 (_tracker_config_path reads all 6 yamls, non-test file); configs/racketsport/botsort_reid_loose.yaml (tracker_type: botsort, with_reid: True — passes validator)
verdicts.explanation:
Both specific factual claims in the audited row are false, though its bottom-line "not wired by default" conclusion is directionally right for 4 of the 5 files.

1. "not read by any non-test .py file in the repo" is false. threed/racketsport/mobile_person_yolo_replay.py (a non-test .py file) has `_tracker_config_path()` at lines 1237-1246 that maps candidate names to all 6 yaml files, including bytetrack.yaml, bytetrack_loose.yaml, botsort_no_reid.yaml, botsort_no_reid_loose.yaml, and botsort_reid_loose.yaml. scripts/racketsport/build_tiled_raw_pool.py:463 also defaults to botsort_no_reid_loose.yaml. These are lane-local benchmark/sweep tools (run_mobile_person_yolo_replay.py, run_mobile_person_accuracy_sweep.py, run_person_detector_oracle.py, build_tiled_raw_pool.py) — confirmed via grep that none of them are imported/called by scripts/racketsport/process_video.py, threed/racketsport/orchestrator.py, or threed/racketsport/remote_body_dispatch.py — so they don't give the 5 files an E2E path, but the auditor's specific factual claim about "no non-test .py file reads them" is wrong.

2. "no wiring or CLI flag surfacing them as alternatives" is false. process_video.py:5139 literally defines `parser.add_argument("--tracker-config", default=str(orchestrator.DEFAULT_BOTSORT_REID_CONFIG))`, which is threaded through to PersonTracker.tracker_config_path (process_video.py:363,1002,4864 → orchestrator.py:487,498,513) — the very lines the auditor cited as evidence for the opposite conclusion. Any of the 6 yaml files can be passed via `--tracker-config` on the real E2E entrypoint.

Tracing what actually happens if you pass the "other 5" via that flag: orchestrator.py:513 calls `_verified_botsort_reid_config(self.tracker_config_path)` (orchestrator.py:2806-2818), which raises FileNotFoundError/ValueError unless the yaml contains both "tracker_type: botsort" and "with_reid: True". Checking file contents: botsort_reid_loose.yaml has both (tracker_type: botsort, with_reid: True) and therefore PASSES the gate — it is genuinely runnable end-to-end via `--tracker-config configs/racketsport/botsort_reid_loose.yaml`, making it OPT_IN_FLAG, not BUILT_NOT_WIRED. The other four (botsort_no_reid.yaml has with_reid: False; botsort_no_reid_loose.yaml has with_reid: False; bytetrack.yaml and bytetrack_loose.yaml have tracker_type: bytetrack) each fail this hard-coded validator and would crash the E2E run if selected — so for those four, BUILT_NOT_WIRED-through-the-default-pipeline is the right end-state, but the audited row's stated mechanism ("no CLI flag") is factually wrong; the correct mechanism is "CLI flag exists and accepts the path, but orchestrator._verified_botsort_reid_config (orchestrator.py:2806) hard-rejects any config lacking BoT-SORT+ReID, so 4 of the 5 variants would error out rather than silently run, and their only functional home is the separate mobile_person_yolo_replay.py benchmark tool which process_video.py never calls."

verdicts.gain_name: Contact-dense (ball_aware) mesh scheduling for hitter-dense mesh in contact windows
verdicts.claimed_status: WIRED_DEFAULT (default mechanism: scripts/racketsport/process_video.py:179 DEFAULT_MESH_COVERAGE_MODE='ball_aware'; threed/racketsport/body_compute.py:273-288 activates contact_dense from frame-plan policy)
verdicts.corrected_status: WIRED_DEFAULT — holds, with one phrasing correction to the caveat (see explanation): it is not "events run after frames" that causes starvation, it's that events runs before BODY/skeleton3d.json on a cold run, so wrist-based contact cues are blocked; ball_inflections/audio cues can still populate contact_windows.json. Even when starved, policy["mode"] stays the literal string "ball_aware" (not "uniform") and still yields a valid tier-1 mesh selection via the in-mode fallback pool.
verdicts.evidence_file_line: scripts/racketsport/process_video.py:179 (DEFAULT_MESH_COVERAGE_MODE="ball_aware"), :343 (PipelineOptions.mesh_coverage_mode default), :353 (body_schedule default="serial"), :492-548 (_run_serial = prefix_stage_fns()+middle_stage_fns()[incl. "events"]+body+suffix — events precedes body), :2270-2278 (_stage_events calls build_frame_compute_plan(**self._mesh_coverage_kwargs()) which passes mesh_coverage_mode=self.options.mesh_coverage_mode), :2353-2359 (_mesh_coverage_kwargs); threed/racketsport/orchestrator.py:2178-2196 (_ensure_body_frame_plan_from_sam3d returns early / reuses existing frame_compute_plan.json when present, so BODY does not regenerate it with frame_rating.py's own DEFAULT_MESH_COVERAGE_MODE="hybrid" default at frame_rating.py:21); threed/racketsport/frame_rating.py:765-766 (policy["mode"]=mode, literal "ball_aware" persists even in fallback), :827-859 (ball_aware branch's own in-mode uniform fallback pool, engaged only when zero contact-attributed frames); threed/racketsport/body_compute.py:273-277 (_plan_uses_contact_dense_profile checks policy.get("mode")=="ball_aware"), :132-146 (_execution_from_frame_plan dispatches to _contact_dense_execution_frames when that's true), :319-325 (applied vs. uniform_fallback_missing_contact_evidence outcomes); scripts/racketsport/remote_body_dispatch.py:147-164 (BODY_INPUT_ARTIFACTS syncs contact_windows.json and frame_compute_plan.json to remote), :1451-1456 ("remote_selection_policy": "honor_synced_frame_compute_plan") — remote path also honors the already-built ball_aware plan rather than regenerating it.
verdicts.explanation:
Traced the real invocation path end to end and could NOT refute WIRED_DEFAULT.

1. process_video.py's PipelineOptions.mesh_coverage_mode defaults to DEFAULT_MESH_COVERAGE_MODE="ball_aware" (process_video.py:179,343), and body_schedule defaults to "serial" (process_video.py:353).
2. In the default serial run (_run_serial, process_video.py:538-548), the stage order is prefix_stage_fns() [ingest, calibration, tracking, camera_motion, placement, (rally_gating), frames] + middle_stage_fns() [ball, ball_arc, events, ball_fill] + body + suffix_stage_fns(). Crucially, "events" runs BEFORE "body" in the default (unmodified) pipeline.
3. On a cold run, _stage_events (process_video.py:2153-2300) fuses contact_windows.json and then calls build_frame_compute_plan(..., **self._mesh_coverage_kwargs()) at line 2270-2278, where _mesh_coverage_kwargs() (line 2353-2359) passes mesh_coverage_mode=self.options.mesh_coverage_mode — i.e. "ball_aware" by default — and writes frame_compute_plan.json to clip_dir BEFORE the body stage runs.
4. When _stage_body later calls orchestrator.run_pipeline(stage="body", ...), BodyStageRunner.run (orchestrator.py:838) calls _ensure_body_frame_plan_from_sam3d, which checks `if frame_plan_path.is_file(): return existing` (orchestrator.py:2180) — since process_video.py's own events stage already wrote it with mode="ball_aware", this early-return means the file is NOT regenerated with frame_rating.py's own function-default of "hybrid" (frame_rating.py:21). That "hybrid" default only matters when frame_compute_plan.json is absent (e.g. orchestrator invoked standalone, bypassing process_video.py).
5. body_compute.py:273-277 (_plan_uses_contact_dense_profile) reads plan["mesh_coverage_policy"]["mode"] == "ball_aware" from that exact plan and returns True by default, which routes body_compute.py:132-146 into _contact_dense_execution_frames — the hitter-dense contact-window scheduling code the gain describes. This is a real, executing code path in a plain no-flag run, not dead code.
6. Remote GPU dispatch (the path actually used for most fleet runs) also preserves this: remote_body_dispatch.py's BODY_INPUT_ARTIFACTS (line 147-164) explicitly syncs both contact_windows.json and frame_compute_plan.json up to the VM, and the payload is tagged "remote_selection_policy": "honor_synced_frame_compute_plan" (line 1451-1456) — so the remote committed orchestrator also reuses the already-built ball_aware plan rather than falling back to its own default.

On the prior auditor's caveat ("starves to uniform fallback whenever contact_windows.json is empty because events currently run after frames on cold runs"): the underlying phenomenon is real but the stated cause is imprecise. Events does run before BODY (not "after frames" causing the problem — frames stage just extracts images, it's irrelevant to contact cues). The actual reason contact evidence can be sparse on a cold run is that events runs BEFORE body, so skeleton3d.json (SAM-3D) doesn't exist yet when _stage_events checks _is_real_sam3d_skeleton (process_video.py:2210) — wrist-velocity contact cues are therefore blocked on a cold run, leaving only ball_inflections and audio cues to populate contact_windows.json. When that intersection is still empty, frame_rating.py's ball_aware branch (lines 827-859) engages its own in-mode "manual_review_mesh_fallback_pool" uniform-stride fallback — but critically, policy["mode"] remains the literal string "ball_aware" throughout (frame_rating.py:765-766), so _plan_uses_contact_dense_profile still returns True and body_compute.py still runs the contact_dense code path (reporting status "uniform_fallback_missing_contact_evidence" at body_compute.py:322-325 rather than silently reverting to a different, non-ball_aware mode). This is a graceful data-dependent degradation inside an active feature, not evidence that the feature is unwired or dead — so it does not change the WIRED_DEFAULT verdict, though the causal chain in the caveat should be corrected as above.
